items.title: Head stabilization behavior and underlying circuit mechanisms in larval zebrafish
items.author: Shin-ichi Higashijima
items.content: Behavioral experiments and neural circuit analyses reveal that larval zebrafish use neck-like body movements to stabilize their head, despite lacking an anatomical neck.

items.title: Representation Transfer via Invariant Input-driven Continuous Attractors for Fast Domain Adaptation
items.author: Dongping Yang
items.content: Integrating modular recurrent networks with input-driven continuous attractors provides a brain-inspired mechanism for invariant motion representation, enabling robust action recognition and fast domain adaptation in noisy, data-scarce environments

items.title: Asymmetry and redundancy of STAT5 paralogs across CD8<sup>+</sup> T cell differentiation states
items.author: Alejandro V. Villarino
items.content: Mouse models are used to distinguish unique and redundant functions of STAT5 paralogs in cytotoxic T cells.

items.title: Quantitative comparison of methods for widespread delivery of small molecules across the blood-brain barrier
items.author: Alan Jasanoff
items.content: Parallel characterization of three techniques for brain-wide delivery of a BBB-impermeable molecule indicates comparative strengths and weaknesses of each method, while suggesting mechanistic contributions to delivery efficiency.

items.title: Genome-wide ribosome profiling reveals a dynamic translational landscape in Arabidopsis seedling roots under simulated microgravity
items.author: Peipei Xu
items.content: Genome-wide ribosome profiling sheds light on the role of upstream ORFs and sequence features in the context of translational reprogramming under simulated microgravity in Arabidopsis.

items.title: Palmitoleic acid promoted by BMPR2 signaling primes CD169 macrophages and alleviates liver fibrosis
items.author: Shuwen Qian
items.content: D169+ macrophages alleviate fibrosis and link BMP signaling-regulated unsaturated fatty acids metabolism with the progression of NAFLD.

items.title: Differential responses of termite gut bacterial and fungal community to tropical forest conversion
items.author: Xiaodong Yang
items.content: Termite gut bacteria remain stable after forest conversion, shaped primarily by host species, while fungal communities shift with both host identity and forest change. This reveals taxon-specific microbiome responses to land-use change.

items.title: Ecologically defined scales outperform grids in models of mammal diversity
items.author: Andrew J. Marshall
items.content: Ecologically informed spatial units improve predictions of mammal diversity in tropical forests by better capturing variation in forest structure, revealing scale-dependent relationships between terrestrial LiDAR-derived metrics and community diversity that are obscured by grid-based approaches.
